finding: canonical_pipeline.md documents that no CLIP-peak-reranking-of-PPR-candidates stage and no predicted-temporal-span construction exist anywhere in iris/ as of commit 1e431b7. IRIS answers from a retrieved frame SET, not a predicted [start,end] interval. The three peak-selection configurations requested by the task (ppr_score_legacy, raw_clip_same_pool, clip_in_ppr_topk) and span construction (peak timestamp, span half-width, boundary clamping) therefore cannot be registered as configuration of an existing stage -- they require new code.

ppr_score_legacy.description: Use existing PPR score (retrieve_ppr output order) as the peak/ranking signal -- this ALREADY EXISTS as the default retrieval behavior (no new code needed for this one arm specifically).
ppr_score_legacy.availability: IMPLEMENTED (is simply the current default L2 retrieval output, re-labeled)

raw_clip_same_pool.description: Re-rank the same PPR candidate pool by raw CLIP cosine similarity only, ignoring PPR score.
raw_clip_same_pool.availability: NOT_IMPLEMENTED -- would require a new re-ranking function taking retrieve_ppr's candidate node IDs and the query embedding, sorting by cosine sim only; must reuse the exact same candidate set (no re-ingestion, no new PPR run) per task requirement
requirement_notes: must use the same PPR candidate set (verify by asserting set(candidate_ids_before) == set(candidate_ids_after))
must use the same query embedding (call _embed_query once, reuse)
deterministic tie-break required (e.g. stable sort by (-cosine_sim, frame_idx))

clip_in_ppr_topk.description: Within the PPR top-K candidate set, select the CLIP-peak frame (max cosine sim) as the single peak; changes peak selection only, does not alter PPR candidate membership.
clip_in_ppr_topk.availability: NOT_IMPLEMENTED -- same new-code requirement as raw_clip_same_pool, narrower (selects 1 peak, not a full re-ranking)

span_construction_placeholders.peak_timestamp: would be the timestamp of the selected peak node -- NOT_IMPLEMENTED (no peak-selection stage exists yet, see above)
span_construction_placeholders.boundary_clamping: clamp [peak_ts - half_width, peak_ts + half_width] to [0, video_duration] -- trivial once peak_timestamp exists, NOT_IMPLEMENTED
span_construction_placeholders.predicted_start_end: NOT_IMPLEMENTED -- no predicted_span object exists in iris/ today

legacy_scattered_topk_ablation.description: min(retrieved_timestamps) to max(retrieved_timestamps) across the full (possibly scattered) top-K pool, registered as an explicitly-named legacy ablation ('scattered_minmax_span') and NEVER the implicit default once span construction is implemented.
legacy_scattered_topk_ablation.availability: trivially computable today from existing retrieved-frame timestamps (min/max), but is NOT wired as an official predicted-span output anywhere -- it is exactly what eval/grounding_scorer.py::iop() already does internally (pred_s=min(ts_list), pred_e=max(ts_list)), which is precisely why that file's IoP is flagged in metric_registry.json as diverging from the official per-gold-span-max protocol when there is no true single predicted span.

no_default_selection_made: Per task instruction, this setup does NOT select a best peak mode or span width. All three peak-selection configs and the span-width sweep values are registered as pending future implementation work, not as executable choices.
